summary: Question 10 of arXiv:2305.15585 asks whether high chromatic number forces some out-neighbourhood to have high degeneracy; the authors explicitly state they have been unable to determine this. The paper establishes a positive analogue for the degeneracy-to-degeneracy setting (Theorem 6: high degeneracy forces a two-vertex out-neighbourhood with high degeneracy), but the chromatic-number version for a single vertex remains open. No follow-up paper resolving the question was found in a search covering literature through May 2026.
search_queries: chromatic number tournament out-neighbourhood degeneracy arXiv 2305.15585
tournament local chromatic number degeneracy out-neighbourhood graph 2024 2025
Barbados 2025 graph theory workshop open problems chromatic tournament degeneracy
Some results and problems on tournament structure Nguyen 2306.02364 chromatic degeneracy
notes: No follow-up found after a 5-call web search. The paper (published in JCTB 2024) itself poses Question 10 as genuinely open, distinguishing it from the degeneracy-to-degeneracy case which the authors resolve positively. The Barbados 2025 open-problems PDF was inaccessible (binary/compressed), and the related survey arXiv:2306.02364 focuses on tournament colouring structure rather than this specific question.
